## Purpose
This file serves as an example configuration for the DiffDoc tool, outlining settings for local and cloud AI model usage, directory structure, and file handling.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will observe the configuration settings when they reference this example file, which will guide them in setting up their own DiffDoc configurations. It includes parameters for local and cloud AI model endpoints, batch sizes, and file inclusion/exclusion patterns.

## Business Rules
The configuration specifies rules for AI model selection, including local and cloud options, as well as batch processing settings. It also defines which files to include or exclude based on glob patterns.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs include the base directory for DiffDoc, AI provider settings, model endpoints, and file patterns for inclusion/exclusion. Outputs are not directly generated by this configuration file but influence the behavior of the DiffDoc tool when executed.

## Side Effects
None identified.

## Error And Edge Cases
Potential issues may arise from an empty `openaiApiKey`, which could lead to authentication failures when attempting to access the OpenAI API. Additionally, incorrect endpoint URLs may result in connection errors.

## Dependencies
The configuration relies on the OpenAI API for cloud-based AI model functionality. Local AI model endpoints are also specified, indicating a dependency on local services.

## Operational Notes
Users should ensure that the specified local endpoints are running and accessible. The configuration allows for concurrency in summarization, which may impact performance based on system resources.
